children.heading: .—Guidance, Assistance, and Oversight
children.label: a .—Guidance, Assistance, and Oversight
children.text: Through the General Services Administration’s licensing agreements, the Administrator of General Services shall provide guidance, assistance, and oversight to federal agencies for the development of child care centers to provide economical and effective child care for federal workers.

children.heading: Allotment of Space in Federal Buildings.—
children.label: b Allotment of Space in Federal Buildings.—

children.heading: .—Child care provider
children.label: A .—Child care provider
children.text: The term “child care provider” means an individual or entity that provides or proposes to provide child care services for federal employees.

children.heading: .—Allotment officer
children.label: B .—Allotment officer
children.text: The term “allotment officer” means an officer or agency of the Federal Government charged with the allotment of space in federal buildings.

children.label: A
children.text: the child care provider applies to the allotment officer in the community or district in which child care services are to be provided;

children.label: B
children.text: the space is available; and

children.label: i
children.text: the space will be used to provide child care services to children of whom at least 50 percent have one parent or guardian employed by the Government; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the child care provider will give priority to federal employees for available child care services in the space.

children.heading: Payment for Space and Services.—
children.label: c Payment for Space and Services.—

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: 1 .—Definition
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “services” includes the providing of lighting, heating, cooling, electricity, office furniture, office machines and equipment, classroom furnishings and equipment, kitchen appliances, playground equipment, telephone service (including installation of lines and equipment and other expenses associated with telephone services), and security systems (including installation and other expenses associated with security systems), including replacement equipment, as needed.

children.heading: .—No charge
children.label: 2 .—No charge
children.text: Space allotted under subsection (b) may be provided without charge for rent or services.

children.heading: .—Reimbursement for costs
children.label: 3 .—Reimbursement for costs
children.text: For space allotted under subsection (b), if there is an agreement for the payment of costs associated with providing space or services, neither title 31, nor any other law, prohibits or restricts payment by reimbursement to the miscellaneous receipts or other appropriate account of the Treasury.
pay accreditation fees, including renewal fees, for the child care facility to be accredited by a nationally recognized early-childhood professional organization;
pay travel and per diem expenses for representatives of the child care facility to attend the annual Administration child care conference; and

enter into a consortium with one or more private entities under which the private entities assist in defraying costs associated with the salaries and benefits for personnel providing services at the facility.

children.heading: .—Reimbursement for Employee Training
children.label: e .—Reimbursement for Employee Training
children.text: Notwithstanding , an agency, department, or instrumentality of the Government that provides or proposes to provide child care services for federal employees may reimburse a federal employee or any individual employed to provide child care services for travel, transportation, and subsistence expenses incurred for training classes, conferences, or other meetings in connection with providing the services. A per diem allowance made under this subsection may not exceed the rate specified in regulations prescribed under .section 1345 of title 31section 5707 of title 5

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: 1 .—Definition
children.text: In this subsection, the term “executive facility” means a facility owned or leased by an office or entity within the executive branch of the Government. The term includes a facility owned or leased by the General Services Administration on behalf of an office or entity within the judicial branch of the Government.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 2 .—In general
children.text: All workers in a child care center located in an executive facility shall undergo a criminal history background check as defined in section 231 of the Crime Control Act of 1990 ().42 U.S.C. 130411See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: .—Nonapplication to legislative branch facilities
children.label: 3 .—Nonapplication to legislative branch facilities
children.text: This subsection does not apply to a facility owned by or leased on behalf of an office or entity within the legislative branch of the Government.

children.heading: Appropriated Amounts for Affordable Child Care.—
children.label: g Appropriated Amounts for Affordable Child Care.—

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: 1 .—Definition
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “Executive agency” has the meaning given that term in , but does not include the Government Accountability Office.section 105 of title 5

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 2 .—In general
children.text: In accordance with regulations the Office of Personnel Management prescribes, an Executive agency that provides or proposes to provide child care services for federal employees may use appropriated amounts that are otherwise available for salaries and expenses to provide child care in a federal or leased facility, or through contract, for civilian employees of the agency.

children.heading: .—Affordability
children.label: 3 .—Affordability
children.text: Amounts used pursuant to paragraph (2) shall be applied to improve the affordability of child care for lower income federal employees using or seeking to use the child care services.

children.heading: .—Advances
children.label: 4 .—Advances
children.text: Notwithstanding , amounts may be paid in advance to licensed or regulated child care providers for services to be rendered during an agreed period.section 3324 of title 31

children.heading: .—Notification
children.label: 5 .—Notification
children.text: No amounts made available by law may be used to implement this subsection without advance notice to the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate.

children.label: A
children.text: the authority granted to the Office of Personnel Management shall be exercised with respect to the House of Representatives by the Speaker of the House of Representatives in accordance with regulations promulgated by the Committee on House Administration; and

children.label: B
children.text: amounts may be made available to implement this subsection with respect to the House of Representatives without advance notice to the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate.

children.label: A
children.text: the authority granted to the Office of Personnel Management shall be exercised with respect to the Senate, by the Majority and Minority Leaders of the Senate, in accordance with regulations promulgated by the Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: amounts may be made available to implement this subsection with respect to the Senate without advance notice to the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.
